dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7097 IH: No American Benefits Abroad Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Feenstra (for himself and Mr. Hunt) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services

form.official-title: To prohibit international wire transfers by public assistance recipients, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No American Benefits Abroad Act.

section.header: Prohibition on international wire transfers by public assistance recipients

subsection.text: An individual receiving public assistance may not transfer money from the United States to a person or entity outside of the United States by means of an international wire transfer.

subsection.header: Requirement on international wire transfer providers
subsection.text: Any person that provides international wire transfer services to individuals shall, before providing such services, require an individual to notify the provider, in writing, whether the individual receives public assistance.
In this section, an individual receives public assistance if the individual receives any payment or other benefit from a means-tested welfare or public assistance program.
